name: Mucuna pruriens
summary: Decision-ready summary: Mucuna pruriens is best framed for dopamine precursor support; Parkinsonian context; mood/motivation context; safety screen: psychosis/bipolar risk; levodopa medications; MAOIs.
description: Mucuna pruriens contains bioactive constituents that support dopaminergic tone, which can plausibly affect mood, motor control, and neurochemical signaling relevant to movement symptoms.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: v9.4: Human evidence involves L-DOPA/Parkinson’s or infertility contexts; block monetized wellness positioning.
claimMap.notes: v9.4: Human evidence involves L-DOPA/Parkinson’s or infertility contexts; block monetized wellness positioning.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Clinical dosing is context-specific and L-DOPA-dependent; not suitable for generic consumer dosing

sources.title: PubMed PMID 15548480. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.citation: PubMed PMID 15548480. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.note: evidence citation lookup; PMID source present in Evidence_Register

contraindications: Concurrent MAOI antidepressants due to a documented risk of hypertensive crisis from combined dopamine and monoamine surge
concurrent levodopa/carbidopa or other Parkinson's medication due to additive dopaminergic effect and unpredictable dosing since mucuna's L-DOPA lacks a peripheral decarboxylase inhibitor
psychosis or schizophrenia due to L-DOPA's potential to worsen psychotic symptoms
pregnancy or breastfeeding or use in children due to insufficient safety data
concurrent antipsychotic medication due to opposing dopaminergic mechanisms that may blunt either drug's effect.
